Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LIN LIU, DBA LIN DEE LIU SERVICES' Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions.

Defendants Lin Dee Liu, Yu Shang Kao, Weimin Liu, and Ami Kimoto's Motion for Protective Order; Request for Sanctions is GRANTED, based upon the following: If more than 35 RFAs are sought, the propounding party must serve a "declaration of necessity." CCP 2033.030(b). The sole ground for such declaration is that the excess RFAs are justified by the "complexity or the quantity of the existing and potential issues in the particular case." CCP 2033.040(a). Excess interrogatories are authorized also on grounds of "financial burden" or "expedience." But these grounds do not apply to RFAs. The responding party may challenge the "declaration of necessity" by motion for protective order, on the ground the number of RFAs is unwarranted. CCP 2033.040(a) Such motion places the burden on the propounding party to justify the number of RFAs served. CCP 2033.040(b). Plaintiffs' declaration of necessity states, "This number of questions is warranted under Section 2033.040 of the Code of Civil Procedure because of the expedience of using this method of discovery to provide to the responding parties the opportunity to conduct an inquiry, investigation, or search of files or records to supply the information sought, and the relative simplicity of this case warrants utilizing this method of discovery rather than taking oral depositions." (Sahae Decl., Ex. G.) The Declaration does not comply with CCP 2033.040(a). Defendants shall provide code-compliant, verified responses to RFAs 1-35 and corresponding FI 17.1 within three weeks of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by e-mail to Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti at peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Enjoin Wrongful Trustees Sale Until Judicial Decree
Off Calendar Aug-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Karen Uchiyama (via CourtCall) for Plaintiff/Moving party VERPRESAR LLC and CAMIERDA I, LLC.
Ravi Sahae (via Zoom for Defendant/Moving party Lin Dee Liu.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: foreclosure sale, underlying action, receiver, property debt, interest, appeal, default, breach, and payments. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs VERPRESAR LLC and CAMIERDA I, LLC's Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Enjoin Wrongful Trustees Sale Until Judicial Decree is DENIED. The following is stricken: (1) pages 12-18 of the 18-page Motion for Preliminary Injunction (no Table of Contents or Table of Authorities) and (2) unauthorized supplemental reply filed on August 16, 2022. Pursuant to the Notice of Motion a Preliminary Injunction is sought per Civil Code 2924.12, 2924.17 and 2924.19. No cases are cited for the proposition that these code sections are applicable under the circumstances of this case; no cases are cited to support Plaintiff's position that any asserted wrongdoings by the defendants constitute a violation of CC 2924.17; and no evidence is provided that any purported violation was material. Order submitted electronically is signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
